---
title: "Project 1 Proposal"
---

| | |
|---|---|
| **Project** | Project 1: Linear Regression |
| **Due** | Week 7 |
| **Weight** | 30% of Project 1 (which is 20% of final grade) |
| **Related Guide** | [Project Guide]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} |

::: {.callout-tip}
## {{< fa book >}} Project Guide
Refer to the [Project Guide]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} for detailed instructions, examples of strong proposals, and tips for choosing a dataset.
:::

## Purpose

The proposal establishes the foundation for your linear regression project. You will identify a research question, select a dataset, and define your variables. The instructor will provide feedback to help you refine your plan before you begin the full analysis.

## Requirements

Your proposal should include the following sections:

### 1. Research Question

State a clear, focused research question that can be addressed with linear regression. Your question should specify the relationship you plan to investigate and why it is interesting or important.

### 2. Dataset Description

- Name and source of the dataset
- How to access the dataset (URL, R package, etc.)
- Number of observations and a brief description of the sample
- Unit of analysis (e.g., individuals, schools, countries)

### 3. Variable Definitions

Identify and describe the following:

- **Outcome variable** ($y$): A continuous variable suitable for linear regression
- **Primary predictor** ($x_1$): The main explanatory variable of interest
- **Additional predictors**: At least one additional predictor or control variable you plan to include

For each variable, report:

- Variable name in the dataset
- Measurement scale (continuous, categorical, binary)
- Brief description of what it measures

### 4. Preliminary Descriptive Statistics

Provide basic descriptive statistics for your key variables:

- Summary statistics (mean, SD, min, max) for continuous variables
- Frequency tables for categorical variables
- At least one visualization (histogram, scatterplot, or boxplot)

## Submission

Submit the following to Blackboard:

- A rendered PDF or HTML document
- Your `.qmd` source file
- The dataset (or a link to it)
